title: Urias Xhaxin

{{Quote|And while [the Jedi] have the Force to aid them, I have two powerful allies with me: arrogance and greed.|Urias Xhaxin|<ref name="DTO">''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide I: Onslaught]]''</ref>}}
'''Urias Xhaxin''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Rebel privateer|privateer]] and [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] working for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. He primarily operated in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]], including the [[Ado sector/Legends|Ado sector]]. His success as a privateer gained him placement on the [[Imperial Most Wanted List|Empire's Most Wanted]] list and a posted [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] of 250,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]].<ref name="Pirates">''[[Pirates & Privateers]]''</ref>

Xhaxin was one of the first to encounter the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] in battle and survive.<ref name="DTO" />

==Biography==
{{Quote|Time to be the sort of man that causes a crew to be so loyal.|Urias Xhaxin|<ref name="DTO" />}}
Xhaxin's background was largely unknown to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance]], though [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|Alliance Intelligence]] marked the man as a "penitent obsessive," unable to forgive himself for past indiscretions. At some point, his left hand was replaced with a [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetic]] prosthesis that concealed a hidden heavy [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]].<ref name="Pirates" />

Sometime before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], he acquired his [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the modified [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|Nebulon-B escort frigate]] ''[[Free Lance]]'', from a [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Arms dealer/Legends|arms dealer]]. The core members of his often-rotating crew were [[First Mate/Legends|First Mate]] [[Khwir]], [[Quartermaster/Legends|Quartermaster]] [[Cotetchtle]], [[Engineer/Legends|Engineer]] [[Rabby Mortunk]], [[Pilot/Legends|Pilot]]/Astrogator [[Kandar]], Gunnery Sergeant [[Mirip Pag]], and Surgeon [[Anet Karl]].<ref name="Pirates" />

During his [[Rebel privateer|privateer]] activities for the Alliance, Xhaxin acquired thousands of tons of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] cargo and several combat starships for the Alliance, and earned from them no less than three [[Letter of marque/Legends|letters of marque and reprisal]]. During his third letter of marque, his [[Alliance observer]] was [[Special Agent/Legends|Special Agent]] [[Hast (Human)|Hast]].<ref name="Pirates" />

[[File:Xhaxin.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Xhaxin analyzes intelligence.]]
At some point during his career, a [[Holojournalist/Legends|holojournalist]] came to report on Xhaxin's activities. According to the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]], the actual work of a privateer disappointed her and she instead constructed a romantic fantasy to peddle. Xhaxin dismissed the [[Holodrama/Legends|holodrama]] the holojournalist eventually had made about him as "purest fiction."<ref name="DTO" />

After the Battle of Endor, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] no longer condoned Xhaxin's privateering, but turned a blind eye to his piracy as they continued to battle the various [[Warlord/Legends|heirs]] [[Imperial Remnant|of the Empire]]. Even following the [[Pellaeon–Gavrisom Treaty]], Xhaxin continued to raid the Empire, concentrating his efforts on unreconstructed Imperials attempting to relocate to [[Imperial Space]]. However, six [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the treaty had been signed, those pickings had become slim.<ref name="DTO" />

In [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], Xhaxin concocted a scheme he hoped would provide enough [[Currency/Legends|money]] for himself and his crew to retire. He allowed a few Imperial ships to go free as they were traveling together, then set up a service that claimed to organize convoys through deep space to the Imperial Remnant. Unreconstructed Imperials eager to journey to [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]] paid well to be allowed to join, but Xhaxin intended to raid the entire convoy at once just before it departed for Imperial Space.<ref name="DTO" />

Instead of a monetary coup, [[Skirmish in the Outer Rim|what awaited]] the pirate captain was a [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Warship/Legends|warship]]. The ''Free Lance'' was nearly destroyed in the opening moments of the encounter; rather than lose his crew, Xhaxin made a blind [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] jump, followed by a second blind jump that wrecked the [[Hyperdrive motivator/Legends|hyperdrive motivators]] and compounded the structural damage. The ''Free Lance'' eventually ended up in a system that was used as a navigational point between Bastion and the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]], where it was discovered by [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]]. Xhaxin [[Second skirmish in the Outer Rim|surrendered]] to New Republic [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Traest Kre'fey]], who promptly had the old pirate escorted to guest quarters and began to debrief him for intelligence on the Yuuzhan Vong.<ref name="DTO" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|We also ran away so we could tell about it.|Urias Xhaxin|<ref name="DTO" />}}
Xhaxin kept his hair and beard neatly trimmed, and only wore white, black, or gray [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]]. Some of his affectations included a [[Corusca gem/Legends|corusca gem]] and [[Mythra/Legends|mythra]] cloak brooch that included an integrated [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], and a gray [[Bantha/Legends|bantha]]-hide [[Glove/Legends|glove]] worn over his left hand prosthesis that earned him the nickname "Grayclaw."<ref name="Pirates" />

Classified as a penitent obsessive by the Alliance, Xhaxin's obsessiveness manifested in his precision, control, and effectiveness as a privateer. While his strikes pushed his ship and crew to optimum performance, he nonetheless kept a reasonable margin of error, and he used straightforward tactics to capture his prey. He ran a tight ship, and followed a strict code of conduct summed up in his "[[Dozen Rules]]."<ref name="Pirates" /> He was devoted to his crew and was willing to take extreme risks such as a blind hyperspace jump in order to secure a chance for their survival against impossible odds.<ref name="DTO" />

Xhaxin was a cunning pirate, able to enact a long-term scheme that centered on creating the false impression a convoy would be safe from his depredations, then setting up a convoy service where unreconstructed Imperials would unwittingly pay for the privilege to be his prey. He was a capable tactician, able to recognize when he was outclassed and successfully judge what actions were necessary in order to survive.<ref name="DTO" />
